title: as_const should be preferred to make a value constant
description: "as_const" should be used to make a value constantAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEsince-c++17clumsyWhy is this an issue?More InfoC++17 introducedas_const: a helper function that converts a value to its corresponding const value succinctly and more explicitly.This is usually done to force an overloaded function call on a non-const object to resolve to the const alternative. Or to instantiate a template with a const type rather than the original non-const one.This rule detects casts that can be replaced byas_const.Noncompliant code examplevoid fn(std::vector<int>& vec); void fn(const std::vector<int>& vec); void constFCaller() { std::vector<int> vec; // Set the content of vec // ... fn(static_cast<const st
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
std::vector<int> vec;
fn(static_cast<const std::vector<int>&>(vec)); // Noncompliant: verbose cast

std::string str = "hello";
const std::string& cref = static_cast<const std::string&>(str); // Noncompliant
compliant:
std::vector<int> vec;
fn(std::as_const(vec)); // Compliant: using as_const

std::string str = "hello";
const auto& cref = std::as_const(str); // Compliant: clear intent

checks.message: as_const should be preferred to make a value const instead of static_cast.
